parent_issue: POL-199 — ANALYZE: Runtime state consolidation, cluster-state design, and repository cognition coverage

assessment.summary: POL-199 is executable and should become an implementation cluster. Runtime truth is currently split across .taskchain_artifacts current-state files, .polaris/runs ledger/archive state, .polaris/bootstrap packets, .polaris/clusters clusters.json snapshots, tracker mutation queues, and telemetry JSONL. clusters.json already has v1/v2 graph semantics and should remain tracker/import truth, while a new cluster-state.json should own live execution state.
key_findings: current-state.json remains required by loop, worker, MCP, finalize, docs-ingest, and tool surfaces; immediate removal would break active runtime paths.
telemetry JSONL is not primary resume truth per the run-ledger spec, but many enforcement and debug flows append it; deprecation must be staged behind ledger/cluster-state coverage.

.polaris/runs/ledger.jsonl is already the committed cross-agent resume index, while .polaris/runs/current-state.json and .taskchain_artifacts/polaris-run/current-state.json currently duplicate active run state.
tracker sync-in writes .polaris/clusters/<id>/clusters.json as imported work graph truth; LocalGraph supports v1/v2 and should not be mutated for worker claim state.
MCP claim/result tools and parent loop already model claim/result concepts, but they write .taskchain_artifacts current-state directly rather than a cluster-scoped state store.
Cognition coverage explicitly skips .taskchain_artifacts, .polaris/bootstrap, and .polaris/runs, and no folder-level POLARIS.md/SUMMARY.md exists under .polaris/* today.

implement_parent: POL-201 — IMPLEMENT: Runtime state consolidation and cluster-state execution model
